headline: Product Leader | 15+ Years Building Workflows, Driving 0-1 Product Development & Global Scale | B2B/B2C Digital Platforms | AI-Focused
about:
My journey into product leadership began not in code, but in media and storytelling. This foundation unexpectedly shaped my approach to building products: fostering a deep appreciation for compelling content and a relentless focus on the user's story.

I found my calling early as employee #35 at Shortcovers, a spirited ebook startup with audacious global ambitions. From those foundational days, I was instrumental in its evolution: spinning off from its parent company (Indigo), rebranding as Kobo, launching its first e-reader, navigating multiple funding rounds, and ultimately, its acquisition by Rakuten.

My 15+ years at Kobo are a testament to my commitment and ability to thrive in dynamic environments. I've had the privilege of taking products from 0-1, building scalable systems, and driving B2C and B2B growth across e-commerce, digital content, and consumer platforms. My approach is rooted in data-driven product development, A/B testing, and seamless cross-functional collaboration, consistently increasing user engagement, retention, and partner adoption. I'm passionate about aligning technology with customer experience to deliver measurable impact, transforming a nascent startup into a profitable global brand.

Always curious about the next frontier, I'm currently deepening my understanding of Artificial Intelligence and Machine learning through ongoing education and daily experimentation. I'm excited to leverage these evolving capabilities to build the next generation of impactful products.

about:
Hi! I'm Deepthi, a Go-To-Market strategist who helps B2B SaaS companies create entirely new market categories and execute the frameworks needed to own them.

My expertise spans the complete GTM spectrum – from strategic positioning through marketing execution to growth optimisation. I don't just build brands or drive leads; I architect market approaches that coordinate both functions for maximum impact.

Strategic GTM Leadership: I specialise in category creation marketing, helping companies establish new product categories rather than competing in existing ones. This approach has delivered 3-5x faster growth, including pioneering India's first "Advisory Accounting Platform" category and transforming companies from commodity positioning to market leadership.

Cross-Functional Expertise: My work demonstrates mastery across different growth models – from enterprise SLG (driving $6M to $9M ARR growth) to PLG optimisation to hybrid approaches that balance self-serve and sales-assisted pathways.

Proven Impact: I've influenced over $40 million in revenue growth through coordinated marketing-growth strategies. Recent achievements include a 30% reduction in sales cycle for high-ACV deals and successful market expansion across multiple industries.

AI-Driven Workflows: I pioneer AI-powered marketing workflows that eliminate traditional resource constraints while improving quality, eliminating entire freelancer budgets and accelerating campaign development from weeks to days.

My experience spans the complete company journey – bootstrapped to funded, local to international, product-led to enterprise-focused – adapting GTM strategies based on business model requirements.

What energises me is seeing companies transform their market narrative through strategic positioning that balances technical accuracy with emotional resonance, particularly for complex B2B products.

When I'm not architecting GTM strategies, you'll find me exploring India's landscapes on my motorcycle. There's nothing like the clarity of conquering challenging terrains on two wheels. I'm equally passionate about motorcycle customisation.

Between GTM frameworks and motorcycle adventures, I'm a proud mom to two boys and contribute to the ISB EE community, helping entrepreneurs refine their market approaches.

I'm based in Hyderabad, where my organised home reflects the same structured thinking I bring to strategic planning.

If you're interested in category creation, GTM strategy, or great motorcycling routes, let's connect!

headline: Senior Product Manager | Leading Platform Modernization for Market-Leading Automotive SaaS | AI Product Strategy | Ex-HP Global Product Leader
about:
I’ve always been a tinkerer and a builder. It’s the thing that gets me out of bed in the morning, and I’ve been fortunate to build products customers love across a few different worlds: automotive performance parts, consumer IoT devices, and now SaaS platforms. Whether it’s hands-on mechanical testing of a new exhaust system or designing an intuitive wide-format printer, I’ve seen the product lifecycle from both sides of the fence.

I started as a Mechanical Engineer, and that "systems thinking" still drives how I build software today. I spent the early part of my career at legacy brands like HP and Mitchell 1, where I learned how to manage global scale and modernize platforms that generate over $100M in revenue.

I’ve developed a real appreciation for the process it takes to run a massive portfolio, but my true passion is for the scrappy side of the work. I believe in using today’s best AI tools to bypass traditional corporate thrash and move from an idea to a working prototype in hours rather than weeks.

A few career highlights:
Platform Modernization: Currently leading the evolution of a flagship automotive SaaS platform with nearly 100k subscribers. I’ve focused on integrating NLP features that cut search friction by 88% and building AI-native workflows to help my team move faster.

Global Product Leadership: Directed IoT roadmaps for multi-million dollar portfolios at HP. I secured $26M in funding for new initiatives and managed product partnerships with international teams like Scuderia Ferrari and Real Madrid.

Technical Foundations: Rose from an engineering intern to a Product Marketing Manager at MagnaFlow, launching 30+ products and using script automation to make our workflows 36% more efficient.

I’m also passionate about helping students and people just starting out in product management. If you are a student looking for a resource or just want to talk shop about the industry, feel free to reach out.

Outside of work, you can catch me surfing, cycling, or driving my GTI at the track. Let’s connect!

experience.description:

The problem: When everything in your inbox looks urgent, you miss the revenue signal. A pricing question gets buried under newsletters. A "need this by Friday" sits unanswered. A deal goes cold because you replied on Monday instead of Thursday.

What we built: Gmail extension that detects buying intent in real time.

It surfaces:
→ Pricing questions
→ Urgency signals
→ Follow-up requests
→ Deal momentum shifts

So you know which threads move money today.

Your CRM tracks your pipeline. Sidekick Log tracks what to reply to right now.

Free to try. Paid tier for real-time alerts and thread change detection.

👉 sidekicklog.com

headline: GTM at Pricelabs | PLG
about:
I build GTM systems where product signals guide human touch.

Over the past 7+ years I’ve led sales development functions across outbound‑heavy SaaS and, more recently, inside PLG environments. That shift changed how I think about growth: signals matter more than scripts, activation matters more than activity, and the best SDRs accelerate product value instead of interrupting it.

At PriceLabs, I run the global SDR function inside a self‑serve, freemium product journey. My work includes:

Developing the SDR Lab OS - a living framework of playbooks, rebuttals and talk‑tracks that scales onboarding and replicates what our best reps do.

Designing signal‑driven outreach frameworks in partnership with Product and RevOps, so we route conversations based on in‑app actions rather than form‑fills.

Running experiments that tie SDR impact directly to activation, retention and expansion; moving beyond “calls booked” to metrics like “listings activated” and “trial nudges completed.”

Exploring Voice AI GTM from pricing models to implementation challenges, to automate simple tasks and free up reps for high‑touch interactions.

Before PriceLabs, I built and led SDR teams at LeadSquared and other early‑stage SaaS, consistently delivering pipeline above quota and codifying scalable onboarding programs.

What’s next? I’m interested in the future of GTM where PLG, product signals, SDR design and AI converge. If you’re a founder, PM or GTM leader exploring this space and want to compare notes, let’s connect.

experience.title: Global SDR Manager | PLG & GTM
experience.description:
At PriceLabs, I lead the global SDR function inside a product-led growth motion, where our users start self-serve and SDRs step in to remove friction, not force meetings.

Key initiatives I’ve driven:

Built the SDR Lab OS: A structured internal operating system documenting workflows, playbooks, rebuttals, onboarding guides, and metrics; making SDR knowledge scalable and repeatable.

Shifted success metrics: Redefined SDR performance beyond “calls booked” to include Listings Activated, Trial Nudges Completed, No Next Steps Resolved, creating cleaner forecasts and better user outcomes.

Product-signal driven outreach: Partnered with Growth PM and RevOps to identify in-app signals (pricing setup, syncs, login patterns, listing counts) that determine when a human should step in. Designed workflows around these signals to tie SDR activity directly to activation and expansion.

Experimentation and GTM design: Launched new workflows like “No Listings” follow-ups, pre-trial engagement, event-based outreach, churn recovery against PMS competitors (e.g., Hospitable). Measured impact of each to prioritize highest ROI activities.

Voice AI exploration: Evaluated 25+ vendors, mapped pricing and implementation models, and piloted automation for simple call use cases. Shared learnings with leadership to inform long-term AI strategy.

Enablement and coaching: Conducted training sessions for SDRs on handling in-market leads, crafted outreach playbooks for specialized segments (e.g., PMI franchisees), and created reports to prioritize accounts and drive higher productivity.

Team scaling: Hired and onboarded SDRs across geographies, built prioritization frameworks, and set up daily reporting loops to monitor activity quality, not just volume.

Impact: Improved trial-to-activation rates, reduced ghosting by aligning SDR activity with product value moments, and positioned the SDR team as a lever for both revenue growth and product adoption in a complex global SaaS environment.

headline: PMM Leader | GTM Strategy through Customer Understanding | Positioning & Messaging | B2B SaaS
about:
👋🏼 I'm Ahnna,

I scale high-growth SaaS companies by making technical product easy to understand. While I have deep specialty in platform-level tech (e.g. the APIs and SDKs that power identity, trust, and global communications), my core focus is always the same: connecting what is being built to why a customer should care. 

I lead with a grounded process:

Customer Insights: I don’t guess. I find the truth by synthesizing qualitative and quantitative data, combining Jobs to be Done (JTBD) interviews, Gong AI calls, and Win/Loss analysis with product analytics to understand why users stay and why they churn.

Strategic Positioning: I use those insights to sharpen our edge in a competitive market. I ensure every new product release has a clear "reason to win," helping the company stay distinct and relevant as the market shifts.
